description: Complete bytecode encoding tables for translating AArch64 assembly into machine code without an external assembler. All AArch64 instructions are 32-bit (4 bytes) wide, fixed-length. Covers all instructions generated by the SASM translator for AArch64 targets, plus the full set of commonly used AArch64 instructions for future expansion.
source_references: ARM Architecture Reference Manual for A-profile architecture (DDI 0487)
ARM ARM Section C4: A64 Instruction Set Encoding

notes.instruction_width: All AArch64 (A64) instructions are exactly 32 bits (4 bytes), little-endian.
notes.byte_order: AArch64 uses little-endian byte ordering. A 32-bit instruction word 0xD2800020 is stored as bytes 20, 00, 80, D2.
notes.no_conditional_execution: Unlike ARM32, AArch64 does not support conditional execution on most instructions. Conditional behavior uses conditional branch (B.cond), conditional select (CSEL), and conditional compare (CCMP) instead.
notes.register_width: AArch64 has 31 general-purpose registers. Using Xn accesses 64-bit; using Wn accesses the low 32 bits (with zero-extension on write).
notes.encoding_format: AArch64 instruction encoding uses fixed bit-field positions determined by bits [31:25] (top-level group) and further sub-fields. The encoding is more regular than ARM32.

encoding_types.R: Register — operation on registers only, no immediate
encoding_types.I: Immediate — instruction includes an immediate constant
encoding_types.LSP: Load/Store Pair — access two registers to/from consecutive memory
encoding_types.B: Branch — PC-relative branch with immediate offset
encoding_types.BR: Branch Register — branch to address in register
encoding_types.CB: Compare and Branch — combined compare-zero and branch
encoding_types.TB: Test Bit and Branch — test a single bit and branch
encoding_types.M: Move wide — MOVZ/MOVK/MOVN with 16-bit immediate and shift
encoding_types.PC: PC-relative — ADR/ADRP with PC-relative address calculation
encoding_types.SYS: System — system register access, barriers, hints

operand_notation.Wd: 32-bit destination register (W0-W30), zero-extends result to 64 bits
operand_notation.SP: Stack pointer (X31 context-dependent: SP or XZR)
operand_notation.Xt2: Second transfer register for load/store pair
operand_notation.simm7: 7-bit signed immediate for LDP/STP (×4 or ×8 depending on size)

SP.description: Stack pointer (when register 31 is SP context)

XZR.description: 64-bit zero register (when register 31 is ZR context)

CS.description: Carry set / unsigned higher or same

instruction_format_bits.description: AArch64 top-level encoding is determined by bits [28:25] (op0). This groups all A64 instructions into major categories.

categories.category: Data Processing — Scalar Floating-Point and Advanced SIMD

forms.description: Rd = Rn + imm — 64-bit immediate
Rd = Rn + imm — 32-bit immediate
Rd = Rn + imm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn + imm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn + imm, set flags — 64-bit immediate
Rd = Rn + imm, set flags — 32-bit immediate
Rd = Rn + imm, set flags — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn + imm, set flags — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn - imm — 64-bit immediate
Rd = Rn - imm — 32-bit immediate
Rd = Rn - imm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn - imm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn - imm, set flags — 64-bit immediate
Rd = Rn - imm, set flags — 32-bit immediate
Rd = Rn - imm, set flags — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn - imm, set flags — 32-bit shifted register
Compare 64-bit: SUBS XZR, Xn, #imm12
Compare 32-bit: SUBS WZR, Wn, #imm12
Compare 64-bit register: SUBS XZR, Xn, Xm
Compare 32-bit register: SUBS WZR, Wn, Wm
Compare negative 64-bit: ADDS XZR, Xn, #imm12
Negate: Xd = 0 - Xm. Alias for SUB Xd, XZR, Xm
Negate 32-bit: Wd = 0 - Wm
Rd = Rn AND Rm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn AND Rm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn AND NOT Rm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn AND NOT Rm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn OR Rm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn OR Rm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn OR NOT Rm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn OR NOT Rm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn XOR Rm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn XOR Rm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn XOR NOT Rm — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn XOR NOT Rm — 32-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn AND Rm, set flags — 64-bit shifted register
Rd = Rn AND Rm, set flags — 32-bit shifted register
Test bits 64-bit: ANDS XZR, Xn, Xm
Test bits 32-bit: ANDS WZR, Wn, Wm
Move register 64-bit: Xd = Xm. Alias for ORR Xd, XZR, Xm
Move register 32-bit: Wd = Wm
Move to/from SP: alias for ADD Xd, Xn, #0 (when SP involved)
Move wide with zero: Xd = imm16 << (hw*16), other bits zeroed — 64-bit
Move wide with zero: Xd = imm16 << (hw*16), other bits zeroed — 32-bit
Move wide with keep: Xd[hw*16+15 : hw*16] = imm16, other bits unchanged — 64-bit
Move wide with keep: Xd[hw*16+15 : hw*16] = imm16, other bits unchanged — 32-bit
Move wide with NOT: Xd = NOT(imm16 << (hw*16)) — 64-bit
Move wide with NOT: Xd = NOT(imm16 << (hw*16)) — 32-bit
Multiply 64-bit: Xd = Xn * Xm. Alias for MADD Xd, Xn, Xm, XZR
Multiply 32-bit: Wd = Wn * Wm
Multiply-Add: Xd = Xa + (Xn * Xm)
Multiply-Subtract: Xd = Xa - (Xn * Xm)
Signed multiply long: Xd = sign_extend(Wn) * sign_extend(Wm)
Unsigned multiply long: Xd = Wn * Wm (64-bit unsigned result)
Signed divide 64-bit: Xd = Xn / Xm
Signed divide 32-bit: Wd = Wn / Wm
Unsigned divide 64-bit: Xd = Xn / Xm (unsigned)
Unsigned divide 32-bit
Logical shift left 64-bit: Xd = Xn LSL Xm
Logical shift left 32-bit
Logical shift right 64-bit: Xd = Xn LSR Xm
Logical shift right 32-bit
Arithmetic shift right 64-bit: Xd = Xn ASR Xm
Arithmetic shift right 32-bit
Rotate right 64-bit: Xd = Xn ROR Xm
Rotate right 32-bit
Load 64-bit: Xt = [Xn + imm12*8]
Load 32-bit: Wt = [Xn + imm12*4]
Load 64-bit pre-indexed: Xt = [Xn + simm9], Xn += simm9
Load 64-bit post-indexed: Xt = [Xn], Xn += simm9
Load 64-bit register offset: Xt = [Xn + Xm]
Load 64-bit literal (PC-relative): Xt = [PC + sign_extend(imm19) << 2]
Load byte unsigned: Wt = zero_extend([Xn + imm12])
Load halfword unsigned: Wt = zero_extend_16([Xn + imm12*2])
Load signed byte to 64-bit
Load signed halfword to 64-bit
Load signed word to 64-bit
Store 64-bit: [Xn + imm12*8] = Xt
Store 32-bit: [Xn + imm12*4] = Wt
Store 64-bit pre-indexed
Store 64-bit post-indexed
Store byte
Store halfword
Store pair 64-bit: [Xn + simm7*8] = Xt1, [Xn + simm7*8 + 8] = Xt2
Store pair 64-bit pre-indexed: write-back Xn
Store pair 64-bit post-indexed
Store pair 32-bit
Load pair 64-bit: Xt1 = [Xn + simm7*8], Xt2 = [Xn + simm7*8 + 8]
Load pair 64-bit pre-indexed
Load pair 64-bit post-indexed
Load pair 32-bit

imm26.note: Signed offset >> 2. PC = PC + sign_extend(imm26) << 2. Range: ±128 MB

forms.description: Unconditional branch: PC = PC + offset
Branch with link (function call): X30 = return address, PC = PC + offset
Branch to register: PC = Xn
Branch with link to register: X30 = return address, PC = Xn
Return from subroutine: PC = X30 (LR). Optionally RET Xn.
Return to address in Xn
Conditional branch: if EQ, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if NE, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if CS, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if CC, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if MI, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if PL, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if VS, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if VC, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if HI, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if LS, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if GE, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if LT, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if GT, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if LE, PC = PC + offset
Conditional branch: if AL, PC = PC + offset
Compare and branch if zero 64-bit: if Xt == 0, branch
Compare and branch if zero 32-bit
Compare and branch if not zero 64-bit
Compare and branch if not zero 32-bit
Test bit and branch if zero
Test bit and branch if not zero
Conditional select: Xd = cond ? Xn : Xm
Conditional select increment: Xd = cond ? Xn : Xm+1
Conditional set: Xd = cond ? 1 : 0. Alias for CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, invert(cond)
Form PC-relative address: Xd = PC + offset. Range: ±1 MB
Form PC-relative page address: Xd = (PC & ~0xFFF) + (offset << 12). Range: ±4 GB

imm16.note: Immediate (Linux uses #0 and passes syscall number in x8)
